draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need high contrast between navigation and content areas to quickly locate client info
facade.label: Dark sidebar, bright cards
facade.acceptImplies: Users prefer visual separation layers; consider dark persistent nav + luminous content zones
facade.rejectImplies: Users may want unified tonal approach; explore all-light or all-dark surfaces instead

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers need high contrast between navigation and content areas to quickly locate client info
evidence.implication: Users prefer visual separation layers; consider dark persistent nav + luminous content zones

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to a friendly, approachable voice that feels like a trusted colleague rather than corporate software
facade.label: Casual, conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: The CRM should use first-person language, gentle humor, and conversational UI copy that makes interactions feel human and supportive
facade.rejectImplies: The CRM should adopt a more formal, business-standard voice with professional terminology and corporate conventions

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond to organic, human-centered interface elements that feel personal and less corporate
facade.label: Handwritten notes
facade.acceptImplies: Explore warmth through natural typography, sketchy UI elements, and human-scaled interactions
facade.rejectImplies: Freelancers prefer polished, systematic interfaces that signal professionalism and trust over personality

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer seeing key metrics (earnings, hours, rates) as individual cards rather than in a data table
facade.label: Numbers in cards
facade.acceptImplies: The CRM should emphasize quick-scan metric cards for at-a-glance financial/project health
facade.rejectImplies: The CRM should prioritize narrative views (timelines, status summaries, client notes) over isolated number displays

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to CRM entry points that animate in rather than load flat
facade.label: Contacts spring to life
facade.acceptImplies: Smooth, delightful micro-interactions should be the default for revealing contact data and task lists
facade.rejectImplies: Instant, no-nonsense rendering is more important than motion—keep transitions minimal

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers benefit from persistent navigation + chunked client/project info in card layouts—reducing cognitive load when juggling multiple clients.
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation persistence (sidebar vs tabs) + information grouping (cards vs lists)
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers anchored nav + visual separation of data units. Likely values quick context-switching and clear boundaries.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer linear flow (tabs/steps), continuous lists, or minimal visual scaffolding. Prioritizes simplicity over persistent access.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers associate rounded UI elements with approachability and trust when managing client relationships, creating psychological safety in financial/business interactions
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Emphasize curves in buttons, cards, and input fields; consider rounded card corners for client profiles and invoice displays
facade.rejectImplies: Explore cleaner, more angular modernism; consider sharp corners and geometric precision to signal professionalism and clarity

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
Whether user wants all tools visible at once vs. hidden behind menus/modals—reveals preference for dashboard density and navigation style in a tool-heavy CRM
Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business-like assistant or a supportive collaborator voice
Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals desired emotional tone while managing client work
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards with stats/charts vs. simple lists and summaries—reveals tolerance for analytics and information density
axes.poleA: Numbers and charts or simple lists?

axes.label: Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, deliberate animations—reveals preference for responsiveness vs. polish in a fast-paced work tool

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants all tools visible at once vs. hidden behind menus/modals—reveals preference for dashboard density and navigation style in a tool-heavy CRM
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards with stats/charts vs. simple lists and summaries—reveals tolerance for analytics and information density
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, deliberate animations—reveals preference for responsiveness vs. polish in a fast-paced work tool
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo
